draft.introduction: Psalm 63 is a psalm of intense longing for God, set in a dry and weary land. Its imagery of thirst and satisfaction became central to the Christian mystical tradition of divine desire.

translation.text: O God, you are my God; earnestly I seek you; my soul thirsts for you; my flesh faints for you, as in a dry and weary land where there is no water. So I have looked upon you in the sanctuary, beholding your power and glory. Because your steadfast love is better than life, my lips will praise you.
translation.original_text: ὁ θεός ὁ θεός μου πρὸς σὲ ὀρθρίζω ἐδίψησέν σοι ἡ ψυχή μου

translator_notes.note: The declaration that God’s steadfast love (ἔλεος) is “better than life” is remarkable—it values the experience of divine love above existence itself. This became a touchstone for mystics like John of the Cross who spoke of the soul’s willing death to self.
